title: 'Lyhanna was a victim twice': Women-led rallies across France condemn failures after schoolgirl's death
subhead: Several thousand people gathered on Monday in front of courthouses and city halls to express their dismay at the judicial treatment of sexual violence committed against children. Many demonstrators called for the resignation of Justice Minister Gérald Darmanin.

title: Trump, claiming to stand between Iran and Israel, seeks to prevent new open war
Paris Mayor Grégoire confronts the dark side of his predecessor's legacy
'The SpaceX stock market debut reads more like science fiction than a realistic business plan'
subhead: Elon Musk is basing the market valuation of his aerospace company, which is set to go public on the Nasdaq on Friday, on the promise of dominating technologies and markets that do not yet exist. In the space sector, however, the line between achievement and disaster is razor-thin, explains Le Monde columnist Stéphane Lauer.

title: Spain faces logistical headache ahead of August 12 total solar eclipse
subhead: In two months, part of the country will watch the Sun disappear behind the Moon. Authorities, having only recently grasped the full implications of the event, are now scrambling to catch up.

title: 2030 Winter Games: Historic French fort will be turned into an Olympic village
subhead: The developer Linkcity, partnering with architect Philippe Prost, will renovate the Fort Trois-Têtes in Briançon, which is to host between 900 and 1,200 athletes.

title: Why Europe is unable to defend its industry against the 'second Chinese shock'
subhead: 'How China is Devouring Europe' (4/4). In recent years, the EU has taken unprecedented measures to combat unfair competition from China. But member states have struggled to develop a more comprehensive strategy.

Alone in Algiers: The activists fighting to preserve the Hirak movement's legacy
subhead: 'Resisting a repressive regime (3/3). Six years after the end of the pro-democracy movement that shook the country, judicial harassment continues to target those still seeking to promote freedom of expression.

title: 'I am a libertarian and belong to no party': The octogenarian, his anarchist friends and the mystery of a bomb at McDonald's
subhead: In July 2025, an explosive device and a torn Israeli flag were left in front of a McDonald's in France. After a 10-month investigation, an 85-year-old former press photographer, who claims to be outraged by the war in Gaza, emerged as the main suspect, while a possible accomplice died by suicide on May 7, just before an anti-terrorism operation.

title: Women's desire, a men's issue?
subhead: No, women should not feel pressured to boost their libidos when they wane, writes sex columnist Maïa Mazaurette, who urges both men and women to reconsider female desire from a societal perspective.
